title: galium vernum var. halleri
intro: Galium vernum var. halleri, also known as Haller's sweet woodruff, is a perennial plant species within the family Rubiaceae, native to Europe and North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a carminative and anti-inflammatory agent, to relieve digestive issues and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Galium vernum var. halleri include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve respiratory health, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including fever, colds, and digestive issues.
